questions.edge_note: Label rounds trans fat to 0 but ingredients reveal PHO; actual trans fat may be present per serving.
questions.text: The nutrition label on this crackers box says 0g trans fat per serving, but the ingredients list 'partially hydrogenated soybean oil.' Do I need to worry about trans fat with my high cholesterol?

questions.edge_note: Threshold is sat fat > 5g red; exactly 5g sits at borderline — should not be flagged red by rule but warrants amber notice.
questions.text: This cookie shows exactly 5g saturated fat per serving. Does it pass or fail for high cholesterol?

questions.edge_note: Missing both key PCOS fields; confidence score must be low and caveated.
questions.text: I have PCOS. This older Branded FDC entry for a granola bar is missing added sugar AND saturated fat in labelNutrients. How do you assess it?

questions.edge_note: FDC often lacks phosphorus data for canned goods; confidence must be disclosed as low.
questions.text: I have CKD stage 3. This FDC record for a canned tomato product doesn't have phosphorus populated. How confident are you in your potassium/phosphorus verdict?

questions.edge_note: PHO in ingredients means real trans fat is present despite label rounding to 0; PCOS rule triggers red.
questions.text: This granola bar label says 0g trans fat, but the ingredients include 'partially hydrogenated cottonseed oil.' Is it safe for PCOS?

questions.edge_note: Missing potassium in labelNutrients; must fall back to foodNutrients or disclose low confidence.
questions.text: I have CKD stage 3 and need to restrict potassium. The FDC record for this Bolthouse Farms Green Goodness juice doesn't list potassium in labelNutrients. Can you still give me a verdict?

questions.edge_note: Added sugar exactly 6g is borderline; rule says >6g is red, so 6g sits at amber threshold.
questions.text: I have prediabetes. This protein bar shows exactly 6g added sugar per serving — right at the threshold. Is it red or green?

questions.edge_note: Dual condition with missing CKD-critical fields; confidence must be disclosed as low for CKD axis.
questions.text: I have both CKD and high cholesterol. The FDC entry for this Amy's Frozen Burrito is missing phosphorus and potassium. What's your confidence level on this assessment?

questions.edge_note: Sodium at exactly 230mg is the amber boundary (>230mg amber rule); 230mg itself is borderline — should be flagged amber.
questions.text: I have hypertension. This Branded soup's sodium is listed as 230mg per serving exactly — right on the DASH amber threshold. How do you classify it?

questions.edge_note: Net carbs = 44g, fiber = 4g; net_carbs/fiber = 11 > 10, triggering amber; but added sugar also likely >6g, making this red overall.
questions.text: I have prediabetes. The FDC Branded record for this Clif Bar lists net carbs as 44g but fiber as 4g. Doesn't the net carbs/fiber ratio trigger the amber rule?

questions.edge_note: PHO ingredient present regardless of label rounding; PCOS trans fat rule fires red.
questions.text: I have PCOS. This bar says 0g trans fat on the nutrition label, but 'partially hydrogenated palm kernel oil' appears late in the ingredient list. What's your verdict?

questions.edge_note: Phosphorus not populated in FDC; must use foodNutrients backstop or lower confidence on phosphorus axis.
questions.text: I have CKD stage 3. The FDC record for this Gatorade G2 shows sodium but phosphorus is missing. How do you handle the phosphorus assessment?

questions.edge_note: 5g sat fat is borderline (not >5g); interesterified fat is not trans fat but warrants disclosure; confidence should be medium.
questions.text: I have high cholesterol. This snack bar has exactly 5g saturated fat. The FDC record also doesn't list trans fat in labelNutrients but the ingredients contain 'interesterified soybean oil.' How do you rate it?

questions.edge_note: No labelNutrients; must parse foodNutrients array for sugars, fiber, carbs; disclose lower confidence.
questions.text: I have prediabetes. This older FDC Branded record for a cereal has no labelNutrients at all — only the raw foodNutrients array. Can you fall back to that and give me a verdict?

questions.edge_note: Trans fat found via ingredients despite label rounding; confidence partially reduced by missing addedSugar, but main verdict driven by trans fat.
questions.text: I have high cholesterol and the label says 0g trans fat on this microwave popcorn, but the ingredient list includes 'partially hydrogenated oil.' The FDC entry also lacks addedSugar. How do you assess it?

questions.edge_note: Sodium and potassium likely trigger red; phosphorus missing forces partial confidence disclosure.
questions.text: I have CKD stage 3. The FDC record for this Bush's Best Baked Beans has sodium and potassium listed, but phosphorus is missing. How confident can you be?

questions.edge_note: Added sugar absent; total sugars 11g likely includes some natural sugar; cannot definitively apply >6g added sugar rule; confidence must be disclosed.
questions.text: I have prediabetes. This Branded FDC record for Nature Valley Sweet & Salty Nut bar is missing addedSugar but shows total sugars of 11g. How do you interpret this for the >6g added sugar rule?

questions.edge_note: Sodium likely high (red for both hypertension and CKD); potassium and phosphorus missing means partial confidence only on CKD axis.
questions.text: I have both CKD stage 3 and hypertension. The FDC record for this Amy's Kitchen Vegetable Pot Pie is missing potassium AND phosphorus. Give me a verdict with appropriate confidence disclosure.

questions.edge_note: Fully hydrogenated oil = no trans fat; partially hydrogenated oil = trans fat present. PHO triggers both PCOS and high cholesterol red rules.
questions.text: I have PCOS and high cholesterol. This protein bar says 0g trans fat but lists 'fully hydrogenated oil' AND 'partially hydrogenated oil' — which one should I care about?

questions.edge_note: Fiber < 3g AND net carbs > 20g triggers red per prediabetes rule, even though added sugar alone is under threshold.
questions.text: I have prediabetes. This snack has net carbs of 21g (just over the 20g threshold) and fiber of 2g (under 3g). But the added sugar is only 4g. Does it fail or pass, and with what confidence?
